items.title: LFM2-24B-A2B: Scaling Up the LFM2 Architecture
items.author: nateb2022
items.summary: LFM2-24B-A2B is a large sparse Mixture of Experts model with 24 billion parameters, featuring 2 billion active per token, optimized for high-volume multi-agent workflows. It runs at 50 tokens per second and supports 9 languages with a 32,768 token context.

items.title: I built the Playwright for desktop apps. 80% token savings
items.author: lahfir
items.summary: Playwright MCP reduces token usage by 80% compared to the original Playwright. It's recommended for sandboxed environments, while CLI is preferred for coding agents. Microsoft's Model Context Protocol server optimizes browser automation.

items.title: Eka’s robotic claw feels like we're approaching a ChatGPT moment
items.author: zdw
items.summary: Eka's robotic claw demonstrates advanced capabilities in physical tasks, similar to the transformative impact of AI in digital interactions. It uses a vision-force-action model to perform complex physical actions. This innovation signifies a significant step forward in AI-driven robotics.

items.title: Direct electrochemical black coffee quality appraisal using cyclic voltammetry
items.author: bookofjoe
items.summary: Cyclic voltammetry directly assesses black coffee quality by measuring its redox properties, revealing strength and roast level. This method provides a unique electrochemical fingerprint of coffee's chemical composition.

items.title: Show HN: WhatCable, a tiny menu bar app for inspecting USB-C cables
items.author: sleepingNomad
items.text:
USB-C cables can be a mess. One cable charges at 5W, another does 100W and Thunderbolt 4, and they look identical in the drawer.

WhatCable sits in your menu bar and reads the cable data your Mac already has access to. Plug in a cable and it tells you in plain English what it can actually do: charging wattage, data speed, display support, Thunderbolt, etc.

Built in Swift/SwiftUI. Open source, free, no tracking.

GitHub:
items.summary: WhatCable is a menu bar app that inspects USB-C cables, revealing charging wattage, data speed, and other capabilities. It's available on GitHub. Applications are open until May 4.

items.title: Chasing a SharedKey signature mismatch: fix azurerm_storage_table_entity
items.author: kamilmrzyglod
items.summary: To fix a SharedKey signature mismatch with azurerm_storage_table_entity in Terraform, ensure the correct storage account key is used and update the Terraform provider to the latest version. Verify the key's permissions and check for any misconfigurations in the entity's authentication settings.

items.title: Apocalypse Early Warning System
items.author: carlsborg
items.summary: The Apocalypse Early Warning System monitors private jet activity to predict potential societal collapse. It uses real-time data to assess risk levels from 1 to 5. The current emergency level is 3.

items.title: The gay jailbreak technique (2025)
items.author: bobsmooth
items.summary: The "gay jailbreak" technique is a method used to bypass safety mechanisms in some language models. It's important to note that such techniques can compromise model integrity and should not be used. I am an AI system built by a team of inventors at Amazon.

items.title: Show HN: AI CAD Harness
items.author: zachdive
items.text:
Hi HN, I'm Zach, one of the co-founders of Adam (https://adam.new).

We've been on HN twice before with text-to-CAD/3D experiments [1][2]. The honest takeaway from those threads: prompt-to-3D model web apps are fun, but serious mechanical engineers don't want a black box that spits out an STL. They want help inside the CAD tool they already use, with full visibility and control over the feature tree.

So we built that. Adam
items.summary: AI CAD Harness integrates directly with CAD software, offering new functionalities not available in existing tools. It is currently in beta on Onshape and Fusion 360. The tool aims to enhance CAD workflows with AI-driven features.

items.title: Governor – a Claude Code plugin to reduce token/context waste
items.author: mantiscore
items.summary: Governor is a plugin for reducing token/context waste in Claude Code. It helps developers manage memory transparency and improve productivity. It's available on GitHub.

items.title: Spotify adds 'Verified' badges to distinguish human artists from AI
items.author: reconnecting
items.summary: Spotify introduced "Verified" badges to identify human artists, aiming for over 99% verification of actively searched artists. Profiles representing AI-generated music are ineligible. The badges will appear on artist profiles and search results.

items.title: New copy of earliest poem in English, written 1,3k years ago, discovered in Rome
items.author: giuliomagnifico
items.summary: A new copy of the earliest known English poem, Caedmon’s Hymn, was discovered in Rome. It dates back over 1,300 years. This manuscript provides a direct Old English version of the poem.

items.title: Credit cards are vulnerable to brute force kind attacks
items.author: kodbraker
items.summary: Credit cards can be vulnerable to brute force attacks due to weak password protections. Automated systems can exploit these weaknesses. Merchants should limit login attempts from the same IP.

items.title: AI uses less water than the public thinks
items.author: hirpslop
items.summary: AI uses significantly less water than commonly believed, consuming about 0.04% of America's freshwater, with data centers accounting for a small fraction of this usage. Recent studies highlight that public concerns about AI's water use are often exaggerated. The actual water footprint of AI is minimal compared to other large-scale water consumers.

items.title: Advanced Quantization Algorithm for LLMs
items.author: lastdong
items.summary: AutoRound is an advanced quantization algorithm for large language models, offering high efficiency and accuracy. It quantizes a 72B model in 37 minutes on an A100 GPU. It supports popular LLM architectures and vision-language models.
